scenario: Next.js SSR render path tracing and request ID threading

steps.step: Orient in the Next.js server architecture

map_quality.rationale: Names all required server classes with file:line (Server at base-server.ts:316, NextNodeServer at next-server.ts:175, NextCustomServer at next.ts:518) and the three subdirectories (app-render, route-modules, response-cache) with concrete paths. Agent can navigate directly to the right server class to extend.

specificity.rationale: Class names exact (NextNodeServer, AppPageRouteModule, PagesRouteModule). Pages-vs-App split is concrete with render.tsx:457 vs app-render/app-render.tsx:3077, plus the selector at next-server.ts:616 branching on isAppPath — not generic prose.

justification.rationale: Subdirectory roles are tagged (e.g., 'RSC + HTML pipeline', 'per-route adapters that call the renderers', 'backing store'), but the WHY of the class hierarchy (Edge vs Node, dev-vs-prod, custom-server seam) is only implied by class names rather than explained.

uncertainty.rationale: Surfaces the DevServer split (next-dev-server.ts) and NextCustomServer seam, and notes the cached-vs-live branch. Does not explicitly flag Edge runtime vs Node, Turbopack vs webpack, or other configuration branches that may change which code path runs.

map_quality.rationale: Walks the full pipeline with file:line at every layer — router-server.ts:226 requestHandlerImpl → handleRequest :371 → invokeRender :298 → NextNodeServer handler → base-server.ts:1852 Server.render → findPageComponents → loadComponents:355 → renderHTMLImpl:616 → lazyRenderAppPage/lazyRenderPagesPage → route-module.render → renderToHTMLOrFlight:3077 / renderToHTMLImpl:457. An agent could insert a hook at any layer.

specificity.rationale: Distinguishes wrappers from inner Impls explicitly (renderToHTMLOrFlight :3077 vs renderToHTMLOrFlightImpl :2568; renderToHTML :1622 vs renderToHTMLImpl :457), and names the outermost entry (router-server requestHandlerImpl) rather than skipping it.

justification.rationale: Explains the Pages-vs-App branch via the renderHTMLImpl :616 selector on renderOpts.isAppPath, and shows what findPageComponents/loadComponents return (`{components, query} including the bundled routeModule`). Could go slightly deeper on why route-modules wrap renderers but the rationale is concrete.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags the cached-vs-live branch (`renderToResponseWithComponentsImpl chooses cached vs live`) and ResponseCache bypass in step 7, and notes streaming/workStore at :3145. Doesn't explicitly call out Edge runtime variants or static vs dynamic divergence as caveats the agent must verify.

map_quality.rationale: Lists the single direct caller with file:line and enclosing class method (`route-modules/app-page/module.ts:160`, `AppPageRouteModule.render`), the indirect call chain through `next-server.ts:635 lazyRenderAppPage`, and two re-export sites — enough for the agent to enumerate change sites. Slight gap: 'build/templates/app-page consumers' are referenced but not pinned to file:line.

specificity.rationale: Caller cited concretely with file:line + enclosing function (`module.ts:160` inside `AppPageRouteModule.render :155-170`), and the export site is named (`module.ts:210 export { renderToHTMLOrFlight, vendored }` plus `module.render.ts:9`). Definition site (`app-render.tsx:3077`) is also given.

justification.rationale: Explains the role of the caller (route-module wrapper) vs the indirect chain (lazy-import via `lazyRenderAppPage`) vs the re-export (kept for static build template), making the WHY of each site clear in the notes block.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags lazy dispatch via `lazyRenderAppPage` and vendored re-exports as items requiring follow-up, and notes Sense reports a single `called_by` edge. Misses explicit mention of edge-runtime variants or dynamic route-module registration that might bypass static analysis.

steps.step: Assess where to thread a request ID

map_quality.rationale: Names RequestMeta at request-meta.ts:51 with helpers at :356/:371/:384, NEXT_REQUEST_ID_HEADER at app-router-headers.ts:49, generation site at router-server.ts:226 and attachRequestMeta at next-server.ts:1954, plus response-cache touchpoints at index.ts:200/310 and IncrementalCache.set:660 — agent can open a PR from this map.

specificity.rationale: Cites NEXT_REQUEST_ID_HEADER with its exact string 'x-nextjs-request-id' and file:line, RequestMeta keys with getter/setter lines, and names concrete test files (client-trace-metadata.test.ts, response-cache/index.test.ts, opentelemetry.test.ts:1483) rather than 'the request metadata system'.

justification.rationale: Explains the RequestMeta-vs-ALS tradeoff ("RequestMeta is preferred over ALS because it survives the lazy ComponentMod boundary" and ALS "only needed if non-request-bound code (after()/cache handlers) needs the id") and the cache-key implication ("MUST NOT enter the cache key").

uncertainty.rationale: Flags response-cache key inclusion explicitly and the cached-vs-live header application at handleGet:310, plus error/404/500 paths. Misses edge-runtime constraints and Middleware (root middleware) as interaction points the rubric calls out.
